summary: The Health Innovation Alliance supports the HTI-5 proposed deregulatory actions, emphasizing the need to reduce burdens on health IT developers. They highlight that removing certain certification requirements will foster innovation and improve interoperability.
key_arguments: Reducing certification requirements lowers development costs and accelerates innovation.
Removing outdated standards like C-CDA creation aligns with current interoperability practices.
Simplifying audit log and EHI export requirements reduces unnecessary complexity.
Less prescriptive USCDI data class requirements allow for more flexible data exchange.
recommendations: Proceed with removing the C-CDA creation certification criterion.
Reduce audit log and EHI export requirements as proposed.
Maintain flexibility in USCDI data class requirements to encourage innovation.
Avoid adding new burdensome certification criteria that hinder developer agility.
notable_quote: Removing certification requirements will foster innovation and improve interoperability.
